dataflow.name: Chicago Shadows
dataflow.description: Compute how many minutes of shadow a Chicago Loop building casts onto each surrounding road segment using a WebGPU shader, then explore the result on a thematic map and a brushable histogram.
dataflow.task: Load OSM Chicago Loop, run a WebGPU shader that attributes per-road shadow minutes from a single building, filter to the segments that were actually shadowed, and explore the result with a thematic map and brushable histogram.

nodes.goal: Load OSM surface, parks, water, roads, and buildings layers for the Chicago Loop.
Run a WebGPU shader that accumulates per-road shadow minutes cast by the first building, hour by hour, on the June solstice.
Filter the roads layer down to only the segments that the building actually shadowed (compute.shadow > 0), so the histogram bins a meaningful distribution instead of one tall zero-bar.
Render Chicago Loop layers with the full road network coloured by accumulated shadow minutes (unshaded roads stay visible for context).
Brushable histogram of shadow minutes across the segments the building shadowed.
